steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank describes a consumer quality that benefits retailers in the subscription model. The passage explains that consumers who stop valuing their subscriptions still keep paying because they don't cancel — the word must capture that tendency toward inaction.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'inertia': Inertia means the tendency to remain at rest — to not act. That precisely matches the passage's description of subscribers who passively continue paying without canceling. ✓
  • Choice B — 'evasion': Evasion implies deliberately avoiding something. The passage describes passivity, not an active choice to dodge a cancellation. ✗
  • Choice C — 'decisiveness': Decisiveness means acting promptly and firmly — the opposite of the passive behavior described. High decisiveness would lead consumers to cancel quickly, hurting retailers. ✗
  • Choice D — 'turnover': Turnover refers to the rate at which subscribers leave or are replaced. The passage is about why subscribers stay (passively), not about a churn rate. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
